module-1-field-notes-repository.label: Project: Start the Field Notes Repository
module-1-field-notes-repository.summary: Turn a small set of neighborhood field notes into a trustworthy first Git repository with a clean initial commit.

try_git_commit.title: Create and Verify the First Commit

try_git_repo_inspection.title: Inspect the Garden Notes Workspace
try_git_repo_inspection.prompt:
Start the module project. In step 1 of 5, Use `pwd` and `ls` to inspect the `garden-notes/` directory. Read the provided `README.md` and observation files before making any changes. Type each command and press Enter.

This practice opens in a fresh workspace. First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter, then type each requested Git command and press Enter.
try_git_repo_inspection.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Use `ls` to see the files available in the directory.

try_git_repo_inspection.instructions:
Use `pwd` and `ls` to inspect the `garden-notes/` directory. Read the provided `README.md` and observation files before making any changes. Type each command and press Enter.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
git init -q -b main
git config user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
printf '.zoeskoul/
main.sh
' >> .git/info/exclude
printf 'Git practice file 1\n' > README.md

try_git_config.title: Configure the Local Author Identity
try_git_config.prompt:
Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 2 of 5, Set the local Git identity for the `garden-notes/` repository. Use `git config --local` to set `user.name` to "ZoeSkoul Learner" and `user.email` to "learner@zoeskoul.local". Type each command and press Enter.

This practice opens in a fresh workspace. First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter, then type each requested Git command and press Enter.
try_git_config.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

try_git_commit.title: Create and Verify the First Commit

intro-to-git-repository.title: Understanding Git Repositories
intro-to-git-repository.bodyMarkdown:
A Git repository is a storage space where your project files and their history are kept. It allows you to track changes, collaborate with others, and revert to previous states if needed. In this project, you'll learn how to set up a Git repository for a neighborhood garden's field notes, ensuring that all changes are tracked and the initial state is preserved.

### Project Brief



**Scenario:** A neighborhood garden coordinator keeps plot observations in a folder, but every revision currently replaces the previous one. The next volunteer needs a trustworthy starting point before more notes are added.

**Your role:** Junior developer preparing the garden field-notes repository

**Workspace:** One cumulative terminal workspace rooted at garden-notes/ with README.md, observations/, and a deterministic local Git identity

**Deliverable:** A five-step repository setup ending with an initialized main branch, tracked project files, one meaningful initial commit, and a clean working tree.
